questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The sound created in a big hall persists because of the repeated reflections. The phenomenon is called
questions.solution:
The persistence of sound in a large hall due to repeated reflections is known as Reverberation.

When sound waves are produced in an enclosed space such as a big hall, they reflect off surfaces like walls, ceilings, and floors. These reflections cause the sound to persist even after the original sound source has stopped producing sound. This prolongation of sound is termed reverberation. It is a common effect in auditoriums and can be both beneficial and detrimental depending on the acoustic design of the space. Excessive reverberation can make speech unintelligible, while controlled reverberations can enrich the sound of music.

Reverberation time (RT) is a key parameter used to measure this effect. It is defined as the time it takes for the sound to decay by 60 decibels after the sound source has stopped.

Mathematically, the reverberation time can be approximated by the Sabine formula:

$$ RT = \frac{{0.161 \times V}}{{A}} $$

where:

$ V $ is the volume of the room in cubic meters.

$ A $ is the total absorption in the room in square meters sabine.

Effective design of acoustic spaces often seeks to optimize reverberation to suit the primary purpose of the environment, such as speech, music, or general sound clarity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Two resistors R1 and R2 arranged in parallel combination in an electrical closed circuit are made of the same material and of the same thickness. If the length of R2 is twice the length of R1, then the total resistance R satisfies

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the current through an electrical machine running on direct current is $15 A$ and the machine runs for $10$ minutes, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is:
questions.solution:
To determine the charge that passes through the machine, we use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The formula that relates these quantities is given by :

$$ Q = I \times t $$

where :

$$ Q $$ is the charge (in coulombs, C)

$$ I $$ is the current (in amperes, A)

$$ t $$ is the time (in seconds, s)

We are given :

Current, $$ I = 15 \, \text{A} $$

Time, $$ t = 10 \, \text{minutes} $$

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds :

$$ 10 \, \text{minutes} \times 60 \, \text{seconds per minute} = 600 \, \text{seconds} $$

Now, substituting the given values into the formula, we get :

$$ Q = 15 \, \text{A} \times 600 \, \text{s} $$

$$ Q = 9000 \, \text{C} $$

Therefore, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is :

Option D : $$ 9000 \, \text{C} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Why a fielder in the ground gradually pulls his hands backward while catching a fast moving cricket ball ?
options: To reduce the acceleration of the ball
To increase the acceleration of the ball
To increase the velocity of the ball
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball.

Explanation:

When a fielder catches a fast-moving cricket ball, the ball has a large momentum. To stop it, the fielder has to bring its momentum to zero.

We know:

$$ \text{Force} = \frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t} $$

where:

  • $\Delta p$ = change in momentum

  • $\Delta t$ = time taken

The change in momentum is fixed because the ball has to be stopped. So, if the fielder pulls his hands backward, the time of stopping $\Delta t$ increases.

When time increases, the force decreases.

Also, since acceleration is related to force by

$$ F = ma $$

less force means less acceleration (actually less retardation).

So, by moving his hands backward, the fielder reduces the acceleration of the ball, and therefore the force on his hands becomes less. This prevents injury.

Final Answer:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball}} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard. It verifies that
options: sound travels much faster than light
light travels much faster than sound
light and sound both travel with same speed
intensity of flash of lightning is very high during thunderstorm
questions.solution:
The phenomenon where the flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard can be explained by the difference in the speeds at which light and sound travel. Light travels significantly faster than sound. Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: light travels much faster than sound

To elaborate, light travels at an approximate speed of $$3 \times 10^8 \, \text{m/s}$$ in a vacuum, and slightly slower in the atmosphere. On the other hand, sound travels at a much slower speed of approximately $$343 \, \text{m/s}$$ in air at room temperature. Because of this large discrepancy in speeds, the light from the lightning is observed almost instantaneously, while the sound waves take a longer time to reach the observer's ears. This delay is why we see the flash of lightning before we hear the thunder.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The image we see in plane mirror is
options: real and thus can be photographed.
virtual and nearer than the object.
questions.solution:
The image observed in a plane mirror is described as virtual and is laterally inverted. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C

Virtual and is laterally inverted.

Explanation:

Virtual Image: A virtual image is formed when the outgoing rays from a point of the object appear to diverge. In the case of a plane mirror, the image cannot be projected onto a screen because the light rays do not actually converge at the image location; they only appear to do so when seen by the observer.

Lateral Inversion: This term refers to the horizontal reversal of the image. For example, if the left hand of a person is raised while standing in front of the mirror, it appears that the right hand is raised in the mirror image.

Virtual images cannot be captured directly on a screen but can be seen by looking into the mirror. It is important to note that a photograph of the mirror itself capturing the virtual image can be taken, but it would not be a direct image of the virtual image.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A glass prism splits white light into different colours. This phenomenon is called dispersion of light by prism. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
questions.solution:
Option B is correct: Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light.

When white light passes through a glass prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to refraction. Each color of light bends at a different angle because each color has a different wavelength and refractive index. Violet light, having the shortest wavelength, is refracted the most, while red light, having the longest wavelength, is refracted the least. This differential bending of light waves causes the splitting of white light into its spectral colors, known as dispersion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the nature of velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration?
questions.solution:
The nature of the velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration is a straight line. This is because uniform acceleration means that the velocity of the car changes at a constant rate over time.

Mathematically, if a car is moving with uniform acceleration, the relationship between its velocity $ v $ and time $ t $ can be described by the equation:

$$ v = u + at $$

Where:

  • $ v $ is the final velocity.
  • $ u $ is the initial velocity.
  • $ a $ is the constant acceleration.
  • $ t $ is the time.

This equation is of the form of a linear equation $ y = mx + c $, where $ m $ (the slope) represents the acceleration $ a $, and $ c $ (the y-intercept) represents the initial velocity $ u $. Therefore, the graph of velocity versus time will be a straight line.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C: Straight line

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the magnification produced by a concave lens of focal length 10 cm, when an image is formed at a distance of 5 cm from the lens?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
An electric circuit is given below. $V_1 = 1 V$ and Resistance $R = 1000 \Omega$.

The current through the resistance $R$ is very close to $1$ mA and the voltage across point A and B, $V_{AB} = 1 V$. Now the circuit is changed to :

where value of $V_2 = 5 V$. The internal resistances of both the batteries are $0.1 \Omega$. The current through the resistance $R$ is about :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \text { Equivalent voltage } & =\frac{\varepsilon_1 r_1+\varepsilon_2 1_2}{r_1+r_2} \\\\ & =\frac{1 \times 0.1+5 \times 0.1}{0.1+0.1} \\\\ \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{eq}} & =\frac{0.6}{0.2}=3 \mathrm{~V}\end{aligned}$

From first circuit, we have

$$ i=\frac{\varepsilon}{R+r} $$

Value of $r$ is very less can be neglected as compared to $\mathrm{R}$

Now,

$$ \begin{aligned} i & =\frac{\varepsilon}{R} \\\\ 10^{-3} & =\frac{1}{R} \\\\ \mathrm{R} & =10^3=1000 \mathrm{ohm} \end{aligned} $$

Now, from second circuit

$$ \begin{aligned} i_1 & =\frac{\mathrm{E}_{e q}}{\mathrm{R}+r_{e q}}=\frac{3}{1000} \\\\ i_1 & =3 \times 10^{-3}=3 \mathrm{~mA} \end{aligned} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the. following figures correctly represents the ray diagram ? (Consider the lens to be thin)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Ray Diagrams for Lenses

A ray diagram is a drawing that shows how light rays move through a lens.

There are three main rays we usually draw for a thin lens:

First Ray: If a ray comes in parallel to the main (principal) axis, it will bend and pass through the lens's focus on the other side.

Second Ray: If a ray goes through the center of the lens, it travels straight and does not bend.

Third Ray: If a ray goes toward the focus before reaching the lens, it will bend after passing through the lens and move out parallel to the main axis.

EXPLANATION:

For a convex lens (one that bulges out):

- A ray that comes in parallel to the main axis will bend and go through the focus on the other side.

- A ray that travels through the middle of the lens goes straight and does not change direction.

- A ray that heads toward the focus before entering the lens will come out of the lens going parallel to the main axis.

So, the right figure for the ray diagram is Option 3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following optical phenomena supports that the light is a transverse wave?
questions.solution:
The phenomenon that supports the idea that light is a transverse wave is Option D: Polarization.

To understand why polarization is indicative of light being a transverse wave, let's delve into the concept a bit more:

Light, as an electromagnetic wave, consists of oscillating electric and magnetic fields that are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of wave propagation. In the case of a transverse wave, the oscillations occur in a direction perpendicular to the direction of the wave's advance.

Polarization refers to the orientation of the oscillations of the light wave. When light passes through a polarizing filter, the filter only allows waves oscillating in a particular direction to pass through. This demonstrates that light waves have orientations perpendicular to their direction of travel, which is a key characteristic of transverse waves.

To summarize why the other options are not indicative of light being a transverse wave:

Option A: Refraction: A phenomenon where light changes direction when it passes from one medium to another due to a change in speed. This is related to the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is transverse.

Option B: Diffraction: The bending of light waves around obstacles and the spreading of light waves when they pass through small openings. While this is also a wave property, it is not specific to light being transverse.

Option C: Interference: This is the phenomenon where two or more light waves superpose to form a resultant wave. It demonstrates the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is a transverse wave.

Therefore, polarization is the phenomenon that confirms the transverse nature of light waves, as it directly shows that light has specific orientations of vibration perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

The mathematical representation for polarized light can be expressed if one considers the electric field vector $$\vec{E}$$ which oscillates in a specific plane, indicating transverse wave characteristics:

$$\vec{E}(z,t) = E_0 \cos(kz - \omega t + \phi) \hat{e}$$

where:

  • $$E_0$$ is the amplitude of the electric field
  • $$k$$ is the wave number
  • $$\omega$$ is the angular frequency
  • $$\phi$$ is the phase constant
  • $$\hat{e}$$ is the unit vector indicating the direction of polarization

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->A car weighs 1000 kg. It is moving with a uniform velocity of 72 km/h towards a straight road. The driver suddenly presses the brakes. The car stops in 0.2 s. The retarding force applied on the car to stop it is
questions.solution:
Convert the speed to m/s:

$$v = 72\ \mathrm{km/h} = 72\cdot\frac{1000}{3600}\ \mathrm{m/s} = 20\ \mathrm{m/s}$$

Compute the deceleration (since it goes from 20 m/s to 0 in 0.2 s):

$$a = \frac{\Delta v}{\Delta t} = \frac{0 - 20}{0.2} = -100\ \mathrm{m/s^2}$$

Find the retarding force using Newton’s second law:

$$F = m\,a = 1000\ \mathrm{kg}\times(-100\ \mathrm{m/s^2}) = -100\,000\ \mathrm N$$

The magnitude is $100{,}000\ \mathrm N$, i.e. 100 kN.

Answer: Option 4, 100 kN.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following scientists mentioned that an electron in an atom could revolve in certain stable orbits without the emission of radiant energy?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Niels Bohr.

Niels Bohr was a pioneering physicist who made fundamental contributions to our understanding of atomic structure and quantum mechanics. In 1913, Bohr introduced his model of the atom, which incorporated some aspects of classical physics and early quantum theories. One of the key postulates of the Bohr model is that electrons can revolve around the nucleus of an atom in specific, quantized orbits without the emission of radiant energy. These stable orbits correspond to certain fixed energy levels.

Bohr proposed that while the electron remains in one of these discrete energy levels, no energy is radiated. Radiant energy is emitted or absorbed only when an electron transitions from one energy level to another. This idea was revolutionary because, according to classical electromagnetic theory, an accelerating charge such as an electron in orbit should continuously emit radiation and lose energy, leading to a collapse of the orbit. Bohr's model provided a way to circumvent this problem by introducing quantum concepts.

Bohr's theory can be summarized by the equation for the quantized angular momentum of an electron:

$$L = n \hbar$$

where:

  • $$L$$ is the angular momentum
  • $$n$$ is a positive integer (quantum number)
  • $$\hbar$$ is the reduced Planck's constant ( $$\hbar = \frac{h}{2 \pi}$$ )

This discovery was critical in the development of quantum mechanics and helped to explain phenomena that could not be accounted for by classical physics alone. It laid the groundwork for future atomic models and a more comprehensive understanding of atomic and subatomic processes.

In conclusion, Niels Bohr's concept of electrons revolving in quantized stable orbits without radiating energy was fundamental in explaining atomic stability and the discrete nature of atomic spectra.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->An astronaut whose weight on the Earth is 600 N experiences weightless- ness on International Space Station orbiting around the Earth. It means that
options: normal reaction of the space- station floor on the astronaut is zero
gravitational pull of earth on the astronaut is zero
space station applies a centrifugal force on the astronaut
questions.solution:
1. On Earth, the weight of the astronaut is

    $$W = mg = 600\text{ N}\quad\bigl(m=\tfrac{W}{g}\bigr).$$

  2. In the International Space Station (ISS) both the station and the astronaut are in continuous free fall around the Earth. They have the same centripetal acceleration towards Earth.

  3. “Weightlessness” means the astronaut feels no contact force from the floor. In free fall:

    – Gravitational force $mg$ acts on the astronaut.

    – There is no normal reaction $N$ from the floor (because both fall together).

  4. Therefore:

    Option 2 is correct – the normal reaction of the space-station floor on the astronaut is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A railway wagon (open at the top) of mass M<sub>1</sub> is moving with speed v<sub>1</sub> along a straight track. As a result of rain, after some time it gets partially filled with water so that the mass of the wagon becomes M<sub>2</sub> and speed becomes v<sub>2</sub>. Taking the rain to be falling vertically and the water stationery inside the wagon, the relation between the two speeds v<sub>1</sub> and v<sub>2</sub> is :
options: $\frac{1}{2}$M1$v^2_1$ < $\frac{1}{2}$ M2$v^2_2$​
M1v1 = M2v2
M1v1 < M2v2
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the problem. Since the rain is falling vertically and the water inside the wagon is stationary, the horizontal momentum of the system (wagon + water) is conserved.

Conservation of Momentum

The initial horizontal momentum of the system is given by: $$ M_1 v_1 $$

The final horizontal momentum of the system, after the wagon has collected water, is: $$ M_2 v_2 $$

According to the principle of conservation of momentum: $$ M_1 v_1 = M_2 v_2 $$

Therefore, the correct relation between the two speeds $ v_1 $ and $ v_2 $ is given by Option C: $$ M_1 v_1 = M_2 v_2 $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
An iron nail sinks in water whereas an iron ship floats. Which of the following statements is correct in this regard?

1. Average density of ship is greater than that of the water

2. Average density of iron nail is greater than that of the water

3. Average density of the ship is less than that of the water

4. Average density of the ship is equal to that of the water

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To understand why an iron nail sinks in water while an iron ship floats, we need to consider the concept of density and buoyancy.

Density is defined as mass per unit volume, and it is given by the formula:

$$ \text{Density} = \frac{\text{Mass}}{\text{Volume}} $$

An iron nail has a high density because its mass is concentrated in a small volume, making its average density greater than that of water. As a result, it sinks.

On the other hand, an iron ship, despite being made of iron, is designed in such a way that it encloses a large volume of air. This design ensures that the overall (or average) density of the ship becomes less than that of water, enabling it to float. The ship's shape and structure cause it to displace a volume of water equal to its own weight before being submerged, allowing it to float due to the buoyant force that is equal to the weight of the displaced water.

Given these points, the correct statements are:

2. The average density of the iron nail is greater than that of water. (Since it sinks)

3. The average density of the ship is less than that of the water. (Since it floats)

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B

2 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A point object is placed at the centre of curvature of a spherical concave mirror. Which one among the fol- lowing would be the correct location of image formed?
options: Between the focal point and the centre of curvature
questions.solution:
1. For a concave mirror of radius of curvature $R$, the focal length is

    $$f=\frac{R}{2}$$

  2. Mirror formula (Cartesian sign convention):

    $$\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{u}$$

    where

    • $u$ = object distance from pole (real object ⇒ $u>0$)

    • $v$ = image distance from pole (real image ⇒ $v>0$)

  3. Here the object is at the centre of curvature, so

    $$u=R$$

  4. Substitute into the mirror formula:

    $$\frac{1}{R/2}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{2}{R}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{1}{v}=\frac{2}{R}-\frac{1}{R}=\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\therefore v=R$$

  5. Hence the image is formed at a distance $R$ from the pole, i.e. at the centre of curvature.

Answer: Option 2 – At the centre of curvature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The human eye is like a camera that has a lens with :
options: fixed focal length and fixed aperture size.
variable focal length and fixed aperture size.
fixed focal length and variable aperture size.
variable focal length and variable aperture size.
questions.solution:
The human eye functions similarly to a camera in many ways. When considering the characteristics of the eye's lens, we need to factor in its focal length and aperture size.

The focal length of the lens in the human eye is not fixed. The eye can change its focal length to focus on objects at different distances. This process is called accommodation, where the ciliary muscles adjust the shape of the lens to change its focal length.

Similarly, the aperture size of the human eye, which is the pupil, is variable. The size of the pupil changes in response to light intensity. In dim light, the pupil dilates to allow more light into the eye, and in bright light, it constricts to reduce the light entering the eye. This process is known as the pupillary light reflex.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : variable focal length and variable aperture size.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Of the following, which does not belong to a nuclear reactor?
questions.solution:
In a nuclear reactor, various components serve specific roles to ensure the reactor functions correctly. Let's analyze each option:

Option A: A turbine

A turbine is an essential part of the secondary circuit in a nuclear power plant. The primary circuit, where nuclear reactions occur, transfers its heat to a secondary circuit, which drives the turbine to generate electricity.

Option B: A heat exchanger

A heat exchanger is crucial in a nuclear reactor. It transfers the heat produced in the reactor core to the secondary circuit that drives the turbine. This component ensures the effective transfer of heat while maintaining the integrity of the radioactive material in the reactor core.

Option C: A mechanism to reduce CO2 emission

Nuclear reactors inherently do not produce CO2 emissions during their operation, as they rely on nuclear fission rather than fossil fuels. Therefore, a specific mechanism to reduce CO2 emissions is not a standard part of a nuclear reactor.

Option D: A reaction chamber

The reaction chamber, also known as the reactor core or vessel, is where the nuclear reactions occur. It contains the nuclear fuel and the control rods that regulate the fission process. This component is central to the operation of a nuclear reactor.

Given the above explanations, the component that does not belong to a nuclear reactor is:

Option C: A mechanism to reduce CO2 emission

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
The electrostatic potential doesn't depend on which of the following 

(1) It depends on the charge

(2) It depends on the endpoints of the path through work done

(3) It depends on the path taken for the work done 

(4) It depends on the amount of work done
questions.solution:
The electrostatic potential does not depend on the path taken for the work done. This is because the electrostatic force is a conservative force, which means that the work done by or against an electrostatic force in moving a charge between two points depends only on the initial and final positions of the charge and is independent of the path taken.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Only statement 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: One parsec in astronomical units (A.U.) is about
questions.solution:
We know:

  • $1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \, \text{light years}$

  • $1 \, \text{light year} \approx 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.}$

So,

$$ 1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \times 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ \approx 20.5 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ = 2.05 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

So, one parsec is about:

$$ 2 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

Correct option: Option 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (Physical Quantity) List II (SI Unit)
A. Temperature  1. Kelvin
B. Weight 2. Kilogram
C. Mass  3. Pascal
D. Pressure  4. Newton

code:
questions.solution:
A. Temperature - The standard unit for temperature in the SI system is Kelvin. ($$K$$)

B. Weight - Weight is actually a measure of the force of gravity acting on an object. The SI unit for force is the Newton. ($$N$$)

C. Mass - The fundamental SI unit for mass is the Kilogram. ($$kg$$)

D. Pressure - Pressure is force per unit area, and its SI unit is the Pascal. ($$Pa$$)

So, the correct matches are:

A - 1

B - 4

C - 2

D - 3

Looking at the options you provided, this combination matches Option 3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following conclusions could not be derived from Rutherford's α-particle scattering experiment?
options: Most of the space in the atom is empty
The radius of the atom is about 10<sup>5</sup> times the radius of the nucleus
Electrons move in a circular path of fixed energy called orbits
Nearby all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus
questions.solution:
Option C cannot be derived from Rutherford's $\alpha$-particle scattering experiment.

Rutherford's experiment led to several conclusions about the structure of the atom:

Option A: Most of the space in the atom is empty. This conclusion was drawn because most of the $\alpha$-particles passed through the gold foil without any deflection, indicating that atoms are mostly empty space.

Option B: The radius of the atom is about $10^5$ times the radius of the nucleus. This was inferred from the fact that very few $\alpha$-particles were deflected back, suggesting a tiny, dense nucleus compared to the overall size of the atom.

Option D: Nearly all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus. This conclusion was reached because the deflection of some $\alpha$-particles implied a very dense center, which contains most of the atomic mass.

Option C refers to the description of electron orbits, which was developed later by Niels Bohr. Bohr's model introduced the concept of electrons in specific orbits with fixed energies, an idea not covered or implied by Rutherford's scattering experiment.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following forces is/are fundamental in nature?

1. Gravitational force

2. Electromagnetic forces

3. Strong and weak nuclear forces

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
In the realm of physics, fundamental forces, also known as fundamental interactions, are the basic forces that govern the interactions between particles and objects. There are four recognized fundamental forces in nature :

1. Gravitational Force: This is the force of attraction that acts between all masses. Despite being the weakest of the four forces, it has an infinite range and is always attractive. It's described by Newton's law of gravitation and Einstein's general theory of relativity.

2. Electromagnetic Forces: These forces act between charged particles and are described by Coulomb's law and Maxwell's equations. They have an infinite range and include electric forces (between stationary charges) and magnetic forces (between moving charges).

3. Strong Nuclear Force: This is the force that holds the protons and neutrons together in an atom's nucleus. Despite its extremely short range (approximately the size of an atomic nucleus, $$10^{-15}$$ meters), it is the strongest of the four fundamental forces.

4. Weak Nuclear Force: This force is responsible for certain types of particle decay processes, such as beta decay in radioactive atoms. Its range is even smaller than that of the strong nuclear force, approximately $$10^{-18}$$ meters, and it is weaker than both the strong nuclear and the electromagnetic forces.

Given this information, the correct answer is :

Option D : 1, 2 and 3

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the correct composition of Borax?
questions.solution:
Borax, also known as sodium borate, is a mineral and a salt of boric acid. Its chemical composition includes sodium (Na), boron (B), oxygen (O), and hydrogen (H). The correct formula for Borax is $$\text{Na}_2\text{B}_4\text{O}_7\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$.

Given the options, the correct composition of Borax is:

Option B

Sodium, Boron, Oxygen and Hydrogen

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The mass of 0:5 mole of N<sub>2</sub> gas is
questions.solution:
1. Molar mass of one N atom (from NCERT periodic table):

    $M(\mathrm{N}) = 14\ \text{g·mol}^{-1}$

  2. Molar mass of N₂ molecule:

    $$M(\mathrm{N_2}) = 2 \times M(\mathrm{N}) = 2 \times 14 = 28\ \text{g·mol}^{-1}$$

  3. Mass of $0.5$ mol of N₂:

    $$m = n \times M = 0.5\ \text{mol} \times 28\ \text{g·mol}^{-1} = 14\ \text{g}$$

Answer: 14 g (Option 2)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Soap with water forms :
questions.solution:
When soap is mixed with water, it forms a specific type of liquid crystal known as a lyotropic liquid crystal. This occurs because the soap molecules, which are amphiphilic (having both hydrophilic and hydrophobic parts), align themselves in water to form various ordered structures like micelles and liquid crystalline phases. The formation and ordering of these structures depend on the concentration of the soap in water and the temperature.

Lyotropic liquid crystals are distinguished by their dependence on the concentration of the solute (in this case, soap) in a solvent (water), unlike thermotropic liquid crystals, which depend primarily on temperature.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : Lyotropic liquid crystal

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: At which temperature does liquid water show maximum density?
questions.solution:
Liquid water reaches its maximum density at about 4 °C, which is 277 K.

• Answer: Option 2 (277 K)

• Reason: Below 4 °C, the increasing prominence of hydrogen-bond–driven open structures makes water expand, so its density peaks right at 4 °C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the chemical formula of Hypobromous acid?
questions.solution:
The chemical formula for Hypobromous acid is:

Option B: $$ \text{HOBr} $$

Hypobromous acid is a weak, unstable acid and is a bromine analog of hypochlorous acid. It can be represented as $$ \text{HOBr} $$, where the hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to an oxygen atom, which is in turn bonded to a bromine atom. This compound is typically formed by the reaction of bromine with water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct order of the valencies of elements Ne, Si, N and Mg?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is known as Milk of Magnesia?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: Magnesium hydroxide.

Magnesium hydroxide is commonly known as Milk of Magnesia. It has the chemical formula $$\text{Mg(OH)}_2$$. This compound forms a suspension in water that appears milky, which is why it gets the name "Milk of Magnesia." It is often used as an antacid to neutralize stomach acid and as a laxative to relieve constipation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which of the following compounds undergoes/undergo thermal decomposition?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: The interconversion of states of matter is a physical change
During burning of a candle, both chemical and physical changes take place
During a physical change, the chemical composition of miolecules changes
Rusting of iron is a chemical change
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: During a physical change, the chemical composition of molecules changes.

Explanation:

In a physical change, only the shape, size, or state of a substance may change. The chemical composition remains the same.

Let us check the other options:

  1. The interconversion of states of matter is a physical change

    This is correct.

    Example: ice changing into water, water changing into steam.

  2. During burning of a candle, both chemical and physical changes take place

    This is correct.

  • Melting of wax is a physical change

  • Burning of wax is a chemical change

  1. Rusting of iron is a chemical change

    This is correct because a new substance, iron oxide, is formed.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a covalent compound?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The chemical reaction : 2AgCl (s) → 2Ag (s) + Cl<sub>2</sub> (g) takes place
questions.solution:
1. Silver chloride, AgCl, is a photosensitive compound.

  2. Under sunlight, AgCl absorbs photons. This provides the energy needed to break the Ag–Cl bonds.

  3. The photochemical decomposition is represented as:

    $$2AgCl(s)\xrightarrow{\text{sunlight}}2Ag(s)+Cl_2(g)$$

  4. In the dark, on ordinary heating or under pressure, this reaction does not proceed appreciably.

Answer: Option 2 – in sunlight.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals does NOT react with cold water to liberate hydrogen gas?
questions.solution:
To answer the question, let's examine the reactivity of each metal with cold water.

Potassium (Option A): Potassium is a very reactive metal. When it comes into contact with cold water, it reacts vigorously to form potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas. The reaction is represented by:

$$ 2K + 2H_2O \rightarrow 2KOH + H_2 \uparrow $$

Sodium (Option D): Sodium is also highly reactive with cold water. The reaction produces sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas. The chemical equation for this reaction is:

$$ 2Na + 2H_2O \rightarrow 2NaOH + H_2 \uparrow $$

Calcium (Option C): Calcium reacts with cold water, though less vigorously than potassium and sodium. The reaction produces calcium hydroxide and hydrogen gas:

$$ Ca + 2H_2O \rightarrow Ca(OH)_2 + H_2 \uparrow $$

Iron (Option B): Iron does not react with cold water to any significant extent to liberate hydrogen gas. The reaction between iron and water typically requires higher temperatures or the presence of steam.

Therefore, the metal that does NOT react with cold water to liberate hydrogen gas is:

Option B: Iron

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The process whereby certain minerals absorb water, expand and change is called as
questions.solution:
The process whereby certain minerals absorb water, expand, and undergo a chemical change is called Hydration.

In geological contexts, hydration is often observed when minerals like anhydrite transform into gypsum. This process involves the incorporation of water molecules into the mineral structure, leading to an increase in volume and a change in the chemical composition. Hydration is distinct from other weathering processes like oxidation, hydrolysis, and carbonation, each of which involves different chemical reactions and agents.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Element X forms a chloride with the formula XCl₂, which is a solid with high melting point. X would most likely be in the same group of periodic table as
questions.solution:
XCl₂ being a high-melting solid tells us it’s an ionic compound with X in the +2 oxidation state. Metals that commonly form MCl₂ salts are the Group 2 (alkaline-earth) elements.

• Group 2 elements → Be, Mg, Ca, …

• From the options given, Mg is the only Group 2 metal.

Answer: Option 3 (Mg)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The number of structural isomers of pentane is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is 3.

Isomers:

  • Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers.
    • Therefore, the compounds that exhibit isomerism are known as isomers.
  • Such structural isomers, which differ in the chain of carbon atoms, are known as chain isomers.

Pentane:

  • Pentane is an organic compound that is a clear, colourless liquid.
  • The odour of pentane is similar to petroleum.
  • It is less dense than water and is insoluble in water. It is also known as quintane and n-pentane.
  • The rotatable bond count of pentane is 2.
  • Molecular Formula: C5H12.

Isomers of Pentane: There are three structural isomers of pentane:

  • n-pentane is the first isomer, which is a straight chain with a normal structure of pentane.

  • 2-methylbutane is the second isomer, which is a branched-chain structure in which one carbon atom is connected to three other carbon atoms.

  • 2,2-dimethylpropane is the third isomer, which is a branched-chain structure with the central carbon atom connected to four other carbon atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following alloys contains a non-metal as one of its constituent?
questions.solution:
To answer this question, let's examine the composition of each alloy mentioned:

Option A: Brass

Brass is an alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and zinc (Zn). Both copper and zinc are metals. Therefore, brass does not contain a non-metal as one of its constituents.

Option B: Bronze

Bronze is an alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and tin (Sn). Both copper and tin are metals. Therefore, bronze does not contain a non-metal as one of its constituents.

Option C: Amalgam

Amalgam is an alloy that contains mercury (Hg) and one or more other metals. Amalgams are commonly used in dental fillings. Since all the constituents are metals, amalgam does not contain a non-metal as one of its constituents.

Option D: Steel

Steel is an alloy primarily composed of iron (Fe) and carbon (C). Here, iron is a metal while carbon is a non-metal. Therefore, steel contains a non-metal (carbon) as one of its constituents.

So, the correct answer is:

Option D: Steel

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:

LIST I

(Chemical process)

LIST II

(Reaction)

A.

Electrolysis of water

1.

Double displacement
reaction

B.

Burning of coal

2.

Combination reaction

C.

Iron nail immersed in copper sulphate solution

3.

Decomposition reaction

D.

Addition of barium chloride solution to aluminium sulphate solution

4.

Displacement reaction
questions.solution:
Let's match the given chemical processes in List I with their corresponding reactions in List II.

List I:

  • A. Electrolysis of water
  • B. Burning of coal
  • C. Iron nail immersed in copper sulphate solution
  • D. Addition of barium chloride solution to aluminium sulphate solution

List II:

  • 1. Double displacement reaction
  • 2. Combination reaction
  • 3. Decomposition reaction
  • 4. Displacement reaction

Now, let's determine the correct matches:

A. Electrolysis of water: This process involves breaking down water ($$H_2O$$) into hydrogen ($$H_2$$) and oxygen ($$O_2$$) gases. This is an example of a decomposition reaction.

$$2H_2O \rightarrow 2H_2 + O_2$$

Match: A - 3

B. Burning of coal: Burning coal, which is primarily carbon ($$C$$), in the presence of oxygen ($$O_2$$) results in carbon dioxide ($$CO_2$$). This is a combination reaction.

$$C + O_2 \rightarrow CO_2$$

Match: B - 2

C. Iron nail immersed in copper sulphate solution: The iron ($$Fe$$) displaces copper ($$Cu$$) from copper sulphate ($$CuSO_4$$) solution, forming iron sulphate ($$FeSO_4$$) and copper. This is a displacement reaction.

$$Fe + CuSO_4 \rightarrow FeSO_4 + Cu$$

Match: C - 4

D. Addition of barium chloride solution to aluminium sulphate solution: Mixing barium chloride ($$BaCl_2$$) with aluminium sulphate ($$Al_2(SO_4)_3$$) leads to the formation of barium sulphate ($$BaSO_4$$) and aluminium chloride ($$AlCl_3$$). This is a double displacement reaction.

$$3BaCl_2 + Al_2(SO_4)_3 \rightarrow 3BaSO_4 + 2AlCl_3$$

Match: D - 1

Therefore, the correct matching is:

  • A - 3
  • B - 2
  • C - 4
  • D - 1

So, the correct option is:

Option C: A - 3, B - 2, C - 4, D - 1

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following salts is responsible for temporary hardness of water ?
questions.solution:
Temporary hardness of water is caused by the bicarbonates of calcium and magnesium.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: Bicarbonates of magnesium

Reason:

  • Temporary hardness is due to soluble bicarbonates.

  • Permanent hardness is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium.

Therefore, magnesium bicarbonate is responsible for temporary hardness of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following acids is predominantly found in tomatoes?
questions.solution:
The primary organic acid found in tomatoes is actually citric acid, followed by smaller amounts of malic acid. However, since citric acid is not one of the choices provided, the best match from the given options is oxalic acid (Option C).

Here is why the other acids do not fit as well:

Acetic acid (Option A): This is the main acid in vinegar and is not the predominant acid naturally present in tomatoes.

Tartaric acid (Option B): Mostly associated with grapes and wine-making, not tomatoes.

Lactic acid (Option D): Typically produced by fermentation (e.g., in yogurt or pickled vegetables), not the main acid in raw tomatoes.

Thus, of the listed options, oxalic acid is the closest answer—even though in reality, citric acid is the most abundant acid in tomatoes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For Aluminium (Al) (atomic number: 13, mass number: 27), which one among the following statements is NOT correct?
options: Number of electrons present in Al is 13
Number of protons present in Al is 13
Number of neutrons present in Al is 14
questions.solution:
Number of electrons = atomic number = 13 ✔

Number of protons = atomic number = 13 ✔

Number of neutrons = mass number – atomic number = $$27 - 13 = 14$$ ✔

Valency of Al is 2 ✘

Aluminium is in Group 13, so it commonly loses three electrons to form Al³⁺. Its usual valency is 3, not 2.

Thus, Option 4 is NOT correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Airbags work on the principle of a chemical reaction triggered by the impact producing a gaseous product that causes a sudden volume change. Which one among the following chemical conversions is responsible for this?
options: Solid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide
Sudden conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option A: Sodium azide into nitrogen gas

Airbags typically use the rapid decomposition of sodium azide (NaN₃) to produce nitrogen gas (N₂), which inflates the airbag. The chemical reaction involved is:

$$ 2 \text{NaN}_3 \rightarrow 2 \text{Na} + 3 \text{N}_2 \uparrow $$

This reaction produces a large volume of nitrogen gas very quickly, causing the airbag to inflate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?
options: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For acid rain, the pH of rain water should be less than
questions.solution:
Acid rain is defined as precipitation with a pH lower than the natural “background” value of about 5.6 (due to dissolved CO₂).

So the correct choice is:

Option 3: 5·6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following processes is NOT part of chemical weathering?
questions.solution:
The one process that isn’t chemical weathering is “thawing.”

• Solution (dissolution), hydration and reduction are all chemical changes that alter a mineral’s composition.

• Thawing (freeze–thaw) is purely mechanical, breaking rock by expansion of ice.

So the answer is Option 4: Thawing.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The reaction that occurs on passing carbon dioxide gas through lime water is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2.

The reaction is:

$$ \mathrm{Ca(OH)_2 + CO_2 \rightarrow CaCO_3 + H_2O} $$

Explanation:

  • Lime water is a solution of calcium hydroxide, that is, $\mathrm{Ca(OH)_2}$.

  • When carbon dioxide is passed through lime water, it reacts to form:

  • calcium carbonate $\mathrm{(CaCO_3)}$, which is a white precipitate

  • water $\mathrm{(H_2O)}$

So lime water turns milky due to the formation of $\mathrm{CaCO_3}$.

Therefore:

$$ \boxed{\mathrm{Ca(OH)_2 + CO_2 \rightarrow CaCO_3 + H_2O}} $$

So, Option 2 is correct.
